(Dutch pronunciation: [[ˈkoːnɪŋkləkə ˈvudbɑl ˌklʏp ˈʋɛstərloː]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Dutch "Help:IPA/Dutch"); often simply called **Westerlo**) is a [Belgian](/wiki/Belgium "Belgium") professional [football](/wiki/Association_football "Association football") club located in the municipality of [Westerlo](/wiki/Westerlo "Westerlo") in the province of [Antwerp](/wiki/Antwerp_(province) "Antwerp (province)"). Since [1997–98](/wiki/Belgian_First_Division_1997-98 "Belgian First Division 1997-98"), Westerlo has been mostly playing in the [Belgian Pro League](/wiki/Belgian_Pro_League "Belgian Pro League"), save for a short stint in the [Belgian Second Division](/wiki/Belgian_Second_Division "Belgian Second Division") between 2012 and 2014. Their highest finish is a 6th place in [1999–00](/wiki/Belgian_First_Division_1999-2000 "Belgian First Division 1999-2000"), [2003–04](/wiki/2003%E2%80%9304_Belgian_First_Division "2003–04 Belgian First Division") and [2008–09](/wiki/2008%E2%80%9309_Belgian_First_Division "2008–09 Belgian First Division"). They have won one [Belgian Cup](/wiki/Belgian_Cup "Belgian Cup"). The only player who has ever been called up for the [Belgium national football team](/wiki/Belgium_national_football_team "Belgium national football team") while at Westerlo is [Toni Brogno](/wiki/Toni_Brogno "Toni Brogno") (7 caps, all of them while at Westerlo).

was founded in the city of Westerlo by students in 1917, and it was named Sportkring De Bist Westerlo. After five years of existence, the club retired. The club **Bist Sport** was created in 1931 and it changed its name to Sportkring Westerlo two years later. At that time. some players left the club to found **Westerlo Sport**, the ancestor of **K.V.C. Westerlo**. In 1939, the club won its [league](/wiki/Belgian_Provincial_leagues "Belgian Provincial leagues") for the first time. Five years later, Sportkring Westerlo and dissident Westerlo Sport decided to merge in spite of the rivalry between the teams, and the new club was called **V.C. Westerlo**.

**[Koninklijke](/wiki/Koninklijk "Koninklijk") Voetbal Club Westerlo** (Dutch pronunciation: [[ˈkoːnɪŋkləkə ˈvudbɑl ˌklʏp ˈʋɛstərloː]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Dutch "Help:IPA/Dutch"); often simply called **Westerlo**) is a [Belgian](/wiki/Belgium "Belgium") professional [football](/wiki/Association_football "Association football") club located in the municipality of [Westerlo](/wiki/Westerlo "Westerlo") in the province of [Antwerp](/wiki/Antwerp_(province) "Antwerp (province)"). Since [1997–98](/wiki/Belgian_First_Division_1997-98 "Belgian First Division 1997-98"), Westerlo has been mostly playing in the [Belgian Pro League](/wiki/Belgian_Pro_League "Belgian Pro League"), save for a short stint in the [Belgian Second Division](/wiki/Belgian_Second_Division "Belgian Second Division") between 2012 and 2014. Their highest finish is a 6th place in [1999–00](/wiki/Belgian_First_Division_1999-2000 "Belgian First Division 1999-2000"), [2003–04](/wiki/2003%E2%80%9304_Belgian_First_Division "2003–04 Belgian First Division") and [2008–09](/wiki/2008%E2%80%9309_Belgian_First_Division "2008–09 Belgian First Division"). They have won one [Belgian Cup](/wiki/Belgian_Cup "Belgian Cup"). The only player who has ever been called up for the [Belgium national football team](/wiki/Belgium_national_football_team "Belgium national football team") while at Westerlo is [Toni Brogno](/wiki/Toni_Brogno "Toni Brogno") (7 caps, all of them while at Westerlo).

The club was founded in 1933, receiving the [matricule number](/wiki/Football_in_Belgium#Matricule_numbers "Football in Belgium") 2024. Their colours are yellow and blue. They play their home matches at the [Het Kuipje](/wiki/Het_Kuipje "Het Kuipje").

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=K.V.C._Westerlo&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

**K.V.C. Westerlo** was founded in the city of Westerlo by students in 1917, and it was named Sportkring De Bist Westerlo. After five years of existence, the club retired. The club **Bist Sport** was created in 1931 and it changed its name to Sportkring Westerlo two years later. At that time. some players left the club to found **Westerlo Sport**, the ancestor of **K.V.C. Westerlo**. In 1939, the club won its [league](/wiki/Belgian_Provincial_leagues "Belgian Provincial leagues") for the first time. Five years later, Sportkring Westerlo and dissident Westerlo Sport decided to merge in spite of the rivalry between the teams, and the new club was called **V.C. Westerlo**.

It became champion of the [Antwerp second division](/wiki/Belgian_Provincial_leagues "Belgian Provincial leagues") in 1960. Eight years later it played its first season at the national level in [Promotion](/wiki/Belgian_Promotion "Belgian Promotion") and it won its league to play the [third division](/wiki/Belgian_Third_Division "Belgian Third Division") in 1969. At the end of the season, Westerlo missed a third title in a row, finishing second behind [Eupen](/wiki/K.A.S._Eupen "K.A.S. Eupen"). The next year, the club was relegated after the playoff and it stayed in the Promotion for ten seasons. The club then underwent two relegations in a row and thus played in the Antwerp second division in 1982–83. Westerlo was then promoted for three consecutive years to qualify for the third division once again. In 1993, the club promoted to the [second division](/wiki/Belgian_Second_Division "Belgian Second Division") and then to the first division in 1997. In 1996, the club added the prefix *[Koninklijke](/wiki/Koninklijk "Koninklijk")* (meaning "Royal" in [Dutch](/wiki/Dutch_language "Dutch language")) to its name.

On 18 June 2019 it was officially announced that the club was taken over by Turkish businessman Oktay Ercan. He immediately made it clear that KVC Westerlo will remain a regional family club, and that more attention will be paid to the social dimension and sporting ambitions. Ercan expressed his commitment in 2019 to a year-long plan called 'KVC Westerlo 2024'.[[2]](#cite_note-2)

## Stadium

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=K.V.C._Westerlo&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Stadium")]

Westerlo plays its home matches at [Het Kuipje](/wiki/Het_Kuipje "Het Kuipje"), which is Dutch for "The Cockpit". Its capacity is 8,035.[[1]](#cite_note-auto-1)
